rationale: The citing paper note centers on product-shape completeness and quantity perception/consumption (e.g., completeness vs. primary-dimension cues), while the cited paper note is about goal-gradient effects in loyalty/reward programs, illusionary progress, and retention. There is no stated overlap in the citing note’s claimed gap or prior-work positioning that would make the goal-gradient paper a clear method/theory cornerstone here, and the raw evidence provides only a DOI-level matched reference without contextual citation text. This looks more like a background mention than substantive use.
